visa_types.processing_time:
Processing time depends on the visa type, the involvement of local authorities in Germany, and the applicant’s individual circumstances. Some applications process within one to two weeks, while others may take several months.
Prior approval: 
To expedite the process, your employer can apply for prior approval from the Federal Employment Agency. Further information is available online at www.arbeitsagentur.de (https://www.arbeitsagentur.de/vor-ort/zav/startseite).
Alternatively, your employer can also contact the foreigner’s office for a fast-track procedure for skilled workers. If you have obtained prior approval from the foreigner’s office, you may contact the visa section directly to arrange an appointment. This does not apply for prior approval by the Federal Employment Agency. 
Applicants should apply as early as possible, but no more than six months before their planned date of travel.
Applicants can rest assured that all applications are processed as quickly as possible. Should additional information or documents be required, applicants will be contacted directly. Applicants are therefore kindly asked to refrain from inquiring about their application status.

visa_types.overview:
Overview

Attention: Booking appointments and handing in long-term/type D visa applications for taking up employment through VFS GLOBAL is currently only possible for applicants habitually residing in the consular district of the Embassy in Beijing (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/Jurisdiction).
Appointments are available for the following visa categories only: 
- Visas for taking up temporary employment as part of an intra-corporate transfer (ICT) or exchange of personnel (Personalaustausch)
- Visas for taking up temporary employment as part of a contract for work and materials (Werklieferungsvertrag)
- Visas for taking up employment as executive staff, manager or specialist 
- Visas for taking up short-term quota-based employment 
- Visas for taking up employment as a Chinese cook/specialty cook
Please note that not all long-term visa categories are processed through VFS GLOBAL. Specifically, applications for blue card-visas and skilled worker-visas are not currently accepted at VFS Global.
Prior approval: 
To expedite the process, your employer can apply for prior approval from the Federal Employment Agency. Further information is available online at www.arbeitsagentur.de (https://www.arbeitsagentur.de/vor-ort/zav/startseite)
Alternatively, your employer can also contact the foreigner’s office for a fast-track procedure for skilled workers. If you have obtained prior approval from the foreigner’s office, you may contact the visa section directly to arrange an appointment. This does not apply for prior approval by the Federal Employment Agency. 
Family members: 
If your family members wish to accompany you, they can submit their application on the same day. Please schedule individual appointments for each family member to ensure a smooth process. Applications for family reunion must be submitted at the same time as the leading applicant’s application and should be booked in the same appointment category. 
If you have already submitted your application at VFS GLOBAL, your family members cannot submit their application at VFS GLOBAL at a later date. They must book an appointment in the “family reunion” category at the German mission responsible for their place of habitual residence.
Please consult the website of the German missions in China to check, whether your visa type qualifies for family reunion. 
For details on required application documents, please consult the website of the German missions in China. Use the information provided for your specific visa type and prepare your application accordingly.
You can find information in German here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-de/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum/1209056). 
You can find information in Chinese here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-zh/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum-1345434).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

Attention: Booking appointments and handing in long-term/type D visa applications for taking up employment through VFS GLOBAL is currently only possible for applicants habitually residing in the consular district of the Embassy in Beijing (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/Jurisdiction).
Appointments are available for the following visa categories only: 
- Visas for taking up temporary employment as part of an intra-corporate transfer (ICT) or exchange of personnel (Personalaustausch)
- Visas for taking up temporary employment as part of a contract for work and materials (Werklieferungsvertrag)
- Visas for taking up employment as executive staff, manager or specialist 
- Visas for taking up short-term quota-based employment 
- Visas for taking up employment as a Chinese cook/specialty cook
Please note that not all long-term visa categories are processed through VFS GLOBAL. Specifically, applications for blue card-visas and skilled worker-visas are not currently accepted at VFS Global.
Prior approval: 
To expedite the process, your employer can apply for prior approval from the Federal Employment Agency. Further information is available online at www.arbeitsagentur.de (https://www.arbeitsagentur.de/vor-ort/zav/startseite)
Alternatively, your employer can also contact the foreigner’s office for a fast-track procedure for skilled workers. If you have obtained prior approval from the foreigner’s office, you may contact the visa section directly to arrange an appointment. This does not apply for prior approval by the Federal Employment Agency. 
Family members: 
If your family members wish to accompany you, they can submit their application on the same day. Please schedule individual appointments for each family member to ensure a smooth process. Applications for family reunion must be submitted at the same time as the leading applicant’s application and should be booked in the same appointment category. 
If you have already submitted your application at VFS GLOBAL, your family members cannot submit their application at VFS GLOBAL at a later date. They must book an appointment in the “family reunion” category at the German mission responsible for their place of habitual residence.
Please consult the website of the German missions in China to check, whether your visa type qualifies for family reunion. 
For details on required application documents, please consult the website of the German missions in China. Use the information provided for your specific visa type and prepare your application accordingly.
You can find information in German here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-de/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum/1209056). 
You can find information in Chinese here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-zh/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum-1345434).

## Visa Fees
VFS Global is collecting three types of application fees:
- Visa fees, which are collected on behalf of the consular authority.
- Application Service fees, which relate to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.
- Added Value Service fees, which relate to
 
optional added value services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/premium-services)
 
you have used during the application process.
Please note: The payment of the above fees does not guarantee that a visa will be granted. The visa fee and the service fee are both non-refundable and non-transferable after the application is transferred to the consular authority, the visa application is denied by the administration or the applicant decides to terminate his or her application.
Visa fees

[ Table: Researcher (Beijing Jurisdiction only)]
The fee is fixed in Euro but payable in Renminbi only.
The exchange rate between EUR and CNY is decided by the Consular Authority and updated periodically, therefore the CNY amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above.
Please note that the visa fee receipt is provided by the Consular Authority and the fee is indicated in EUR and CNY.
Application service fee
VFS GLOBAL Service fee mandatory for all visa applications is 34.13 EUR, 273 CNY per application inclusive of taxes. This fee corresponds to the standard application processing service provided by VFS (i.e. Information provision, appointment booking, visa application reception, data processing, etc.).

- 
## Visa fee exemptions
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Spouse, partner in civil union or underage child of German citizen
- Parents of underage German citizen
- Family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement
- Applicants who receive a publicly funded German scholarship for their stay in Germany
- 
## Mode of Payment
Mode of Payment:
- Counters accept payments by Cash/WeChat/POS/Alipay

## Documents Required
For details on required application documents, please consult the website of the German missions in China. Use the information provided for your specific visa type and prepare your application accordingly.
You can find information in German here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-de/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum/1209056). 
You can find information in Chinese here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-zh/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum-1345434) .

## Photo Specifications
Photo
Please submit one passport photo which should fulfil the following criteria (conforming to ICAO standards).
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
                                                                    
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
- Photo should not be modified with Photoshop
The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfil the above criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area.
                                                                        For more details, please refer to the photo template. 
Photos that are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. To avoid
                                                                        this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
Processing time depends on the visa type, the involvement of local authorities in Germany, and the applicant’s individual circumstances. Some applications process within one to two weeks, while others may take several months.
Prior approval: 
To expedite the process, your employer can apply for prior approval from the Federal Employment Agency. Further information is available online at www.arbeitsagentur.de (https://www.arbeitsagentur.de/vor-ort/zav/startseite).
Alternatively, your employer can also contact the foreigner’s office for a fast-track procedure for skilled workers. If you have obtained prior approval from the foreigner’s office, you may contact the visa section directly to arrange an appointment. This does not apply for prior approval by the Federal Employment Agency. 
Applicants should apply as early as possible, but no more than six months before their planned date of travel.
Applicants can rest assured that all applications are processed as quickly as possible. Should additional information or documents be required, applicants will be contacted directly. Applicants are therefore kindly asked to refrain from inquiring about their application status.

## Download Forms
National/long-term Visa Application Form
Please complete and print the following application form* (https://videx-national.diplo.de/ (https://videx-national.diplo.de/)). Sign and submit  it with your supporting visa application documents. For more information, read  the user guide.
The application form has to be signed personally by the applicant (as  the applicant has signed their passport). For applicants under 18 years of age,  the application form must be signed by all parents or legal guardians.
Please make sure you have provided a valid e-mail address and phone  number in the application form. All further information regarding your visa  application (e.g., requests for additional documents/information) will be sent  to your e-mail address mentioned in the application form.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS GLOBAL must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are hired to work on a ship anchored at a German port for further sailing in international waters, please apply for your seaman visa covering the days you need to transfer onto the ship. 
If you intend to be trained on a cruise ship before its sailing off, please apply your Schengen visa for the duration of your training, max. 90 days.
Important: Your documents must contain the name of the ship and the IMO-Number.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are hired to work on a ship anchored at a German port for further sailing in international waters, please apply for your seaman visa covering the days you need to transfer onto the ship. 
If you intend to be trained on a cruise ship before its sailing off, please apply your Schengen visa for the duration of your training, max. 90 days.
Important: Your documents must contain the name of the ship and the IMO-Number.

## Visa Fees
VFS Global is collecting three types of application fees:
- Visa fees, which are collected on behalf of the consular authority.
- Application Service fees, which relate to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.
- Added Value Service fees, which relate to optional added value services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/premium-services) you have used during the application process.
Please note: The payment of the above fees does not guarantee that a visa will be granted. The exchange rate between EUR and CNY is decided by the Consular Authority and updated periodically, therefore the CNY amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above.
Please note that the visa fee receipt is provided by the Consular Authority and the fee is indicated in EUR and CNY.
Visa fee of 280 CNY is stipulated by a visa facilitation agreement with the EU for citizens of: Belarus, Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia Herzegovina, Georgia, Macedonia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia and Ukraine.
Application service fee
The service fee, mandatory for all visa applications is 19.13 EUR, RMB 153 CNY per application inclusive of taxes. This fee corresponds to the standard application processing service provided by VFS (i.e. Information provision, appointment booking, visa application reception, data processing, etc.).
Added Value Service fees
For more information, please check the Premium services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/premium-services) page.
- 
## Visa fee exemptions
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Spouse, partner in civil union or underage child of German citizen
- Parents of underage German citizen
- Family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement
- School pupils, students, postgraduates students and accompanying teachers for study or educational training, if part of the local curriculum (proof has to be provided)
- Researchers traveling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Representatives of registered non-profit organizations aged max. 25 years for events organized by registered non-profit organizations
- Exhibitors at German trade fairs holding an official exhibitor badge
- Persons officially invited by a German public institution when travel and accommodation expenses are fully covered by German public funds

## Photo Specifications
Please submit one passport photo which should fulfil the following criteria ( conforming to ICAO standards). 
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)

The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfil the above criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area. For more details, please refer to the photo template. 
Photos that are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. To avoid
                                                                        this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
Normal processing times are within a few working days. However, processing times vary from application to application at the sole discretion of the German mission, and some applications
                                                                        may take longer than others to get processed due to their complexity. Please check that the visa application form is complete and accurate.
                                                                    
Please submit your visa application form and relevant supporting documents at least 15 days before the intended departure.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you plan a sightseeing tour to Germany as a tourist, a Schengen visa is the correct visa type to apply for. Please submit all documents to prove tourism as the purpose of your travel.
                                                                        Please enclose information about the tour operator/travel agency who is organising the trip. If you are travelling with a group, family members, or friends, please state this information
                                                                        in the travel programme.
Those seeking to visit or exhibit in a trade fair should follow the document list for a trade fair to prepare their application.
This visa type also applies to spouses/children accompanying their spouse/parent who hold a valid business visa.
Do not apply for a tourism visa if you are travelling for business purposes (#business) or visit family/friends (#visit-family). Applying for the incorrect travel purpose and providing false or falsified documents
                                                                        may lead to the refusal of your application. You must submit an itinerary and proof of accommodation that is valid and corresponds to your travel plans.
- ADS-group travel is subject to a special application process. Please contact your tour operator for more information.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you plan a sightseeing tour to Germany as a tourist, a Schengen visa is the correct visa type to apply for. Please submit all documents to prove tourism as the purpose of your travel.
                                                                        Please enclose information about the tour operator/travel agency who is organising the trip. If you are travelling with a group, family members, or friends, please state this information
                                                                        in the travel programme.
Those seeking to visit or exhibit in a trade fair should follow the document list for a trade fair to prepare their application.
This visa type also applies to spouses/children accompanying their spouse/parent who hold a valid business visa.
Do not apply for a tourism visa if you are travelling for business purposes (#business) or visit family/friends (#visit-family). Applying for the incorrect travel purpose and providing false or falsified documents
                                                                        may lead to the refusal of your application. You must submit an itinerary and proof of accommodation that is valid and corresponds to your travel plans.
- ADS-group travel is subject to a special application process. Please contact your tour operator for more information.

## Visa Fees
VFS Global is collecting three types of application fees:

- Application Service fees, which relate to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.

## Visa fee exemptions

- Spouse, partner in civil union or underage child of German citizen
- Parents of underage German citizen
- Family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement
- School pupils, students, postgraduates students and accompanying teachers for study or educational training, if part of the local curriculum (proof has to be provided)
- Researchers traveling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research

Information Leaflets

- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm

- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa category applies to professionals or students who received an invitation to participate in a cultural or sports event in Germany.
If you participate in individual cultural or sporting events as part of a mainly tourist youth and/or group travel, please apply for a tourism visa (#tourism) and submit additional documents about
                                                                        the event. Examples are child or youth camps followed by a tourism programme, participation at music competitions followed by a round-trip, etc. Please always attach detailed documents
                                                                        about the programme and the organiser of the cultural or sporting event. If the journey is organised by a Chinese or German intermediary company, this information should be mentioned
                                                                        in your documents.
Employment as an artist/musician/actor/acrobat or Taiji-trainer etc. can be allowed in Germany without a formal work permit if not exceeding 90 days within one year (for daily performances
                                                                        if not exceeding 15 days within one year). Please provide a copy of your signed contract and a detailed event schedule.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Travellers with confirmed clinical appointments in Germany can apply for a Schengen visa for medical treatment. Special provisions for your travel medical insurance might apply.
This visa category also applies to accompanying family members, who should provide proof of accommodation as well. It is important to submit a detailed letter issued by the hospital or
                                                                        the physician containing details about the planned period of your stay and estimated costs. You need to provide proof that all costs will be covered.
If the nature of your stay at a German clinic is mainly tourist sightseeing and SPA, please apply for a  tourism visa (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/china/english/#tourism).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business travelers who conduct meetings or negotiations in Germany for an employer who is based in a location outside Germany (abroad), draw up contractual offers, conclude contracts or monitor the performance of a contractual agreement should apply for a business visa. Those who conduct market research, short-stay scientific research, in-company continuing education and training in the German part of the company, participate in conferences and workshops, attend job interviews or engage in any other professional business activity should also apply for a business visa.
- Please provide relevant details in the invitation and employment letter if your travel purpose is related to contracts for work and materials (Werklieferungsverträge), e.g. pre-acceptance
                                                                            of machines ordered by a supplier in Germany.
- Employees who are posted to Germany by their employer for after-sales services of machines or equipment delivered to Germany, approval by the Federal Employment Agency (Bundesagentur
                                                                            für Arbeit) must be provided with your application.
- Frequent business travelers are encouraged to apply for a multiple-entry visa valid for one to five years, provided that the applicant has obtained and lawfully used previous visas
                                                                            in recent years. Applicants can enclose their request for a multi-year visa with an informal letter which can be added to the application documents.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you plan to visit family members or friends in Germany, the visitor visa is the correct visa type.
- For family visits, please submit a notary certificate of family relationship. Chinese documents have to be legalized by the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Other foreign documents
                                                                            might need legalization or apostille. The original document will be returned with the passport and can be reused for future visa applications.
- If the invitation letter is signed by one of your in-law-relatives (e.g. son-in-law or daughter-in-law), please also provide relevant proof of family relationship and a copy of the
                                                                            passport and, if applicable, a copy of the German residence permit of your direct relative.
- Frequent travelers are encouraged to apply for a multiple-entry visitor visa valid for one to five years, provided that the applicant has obtained and lawfully used previous visas
                                                                            in recent years. Applicants can enclose their request for a multi-year visa with an informal letter which can be added to the application documents.

Overview
You can apply for this visa type if you are:
- The spouse or partner in civil union of a German citizen.
- The parent of a child (under 18 years old) holding German nationality.
Please note that a Schengen visa only allows you to stay in Germany for a maximum of 90 days within a period of 180 days. It is not possible to change a Schengen visa into a permanent
                                                                        residence permit upon arrival. If you are intending a permanent stay in Germany, please apply for a national visa (https://china.diplo.de/) at the respective German mission.
Frequent travelers are encouraged to apply for a multiple-entry visitor visa valid for one to five years, provided that the applicant has obtained and lawfully used previous visas in
                                                                        recent years. Applicants can enclose their request for a multi-year visa with an informal letter which can be added to the application documents.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Attention: Booking appointments and handing in long-term/type D visa applications through VFS is currently only possible for applicants who have their habitual residence in the consular district of the Embassy in Beijing, the Consulate General in Shanghai and Guangzhou (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/Jurisdiction).
Foreigners can be granted a visa to attend (high) school in Germany. The  following requirements apply:
- school attendance from the 9th grade or higher
- classes must be made up of pupils of different  nationalities
- education costs (tuition, housing fee, etc.) are borne  by the parents
- it must be school that leads to a higher education  entrance qualification (e.g. Abitur) or a comparable qualification
- the students are generally accommodated in a boarding  school belonging to the school
If a language course of more than 90 days is to  precede the school attendance, a visa for the language course can be applied  for initially.

- Visa fees, which are collected on behalf of the consular authority.
- Application Service fees, which relate to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.
- Added Value Service fees, which relate to
 
optional added value services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/premium-services)
 
you have used during the application process.
Please note: The payment of the above fees does not guarantee that a visa will be granted. The visa fee and the service fee are both non-refundable and non-transferable after the application is transferred to the consular authority, the visa application is denied by the administration or the applicant decides to terminate his or her application.
Visa fees

[ Table: Researcher (Beijing Jurisdiction only)]
The fee is fixed in Euro but payable in Renminbi only.
The exchange rate between EUR and CNY is decided by the Consular Authority and updated periodically, therefore the CNY amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above.
Please note that the visa fee receipt is provided by the Consular Authority and the fee is indicated in EUR and CNY.
Application service fee
VFS GLOBAL Service fee mandatory for all visa applications is 34.13 EUR, 273 CNY per application inclusive of taxes. This fee corresponds to the standard application processing service provided by VFS (i.e. Information provision, appointment booking, visa application reception, data processing, etc.).

- 
## Visa fee exemptions
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Spouse, partner in civil union or underage child of German citizen
- Parents of underage German citizen
- Family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement
- Applicants who receive a publicly funded German scholarship for their stay in Germany
- 
## Mode of Payment
Mode of Payment:
- Counters accept payments by Cash/WeChat/POS/Alipay

## Documents Required
For details on required application documents, please consult the website of the German missions in China. Use the information provided for your specific visa type and prepare your application accordingly.
You can find information in German here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-de/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum/1209056). 
You can find information in Chinese here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-zh/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum-1345434) .

## Photo Specifications
Photo
Please submit one passport photo which should fulfil the following criteria (conforming to ICAO standards).
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
                                                                    
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
- Photo should not be modified with Photoshop
The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfil the above criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area.
                                                                        For more details, please refer to the photo template. 
Photos that are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. To avoid
                                                                        this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
Processing time depends on the visa type, the involvement of local authorities in Germany, and the applicant’s individual circumstances. Some applications process within one to two weeks, while others may take several months.
Applicants should apply as early as possible, but no more than six months before their planned date of travel.
Applicants can rest assured that all applications are processed as quickly as possible. Should additional information or documents be required, applicants will be contacted directly. Applicants are therefore kindly asked to refrain from inquiring about their application status.

Please complete and print the following application form* (https://videx-national.diplo.de/ (https://videx-national.diplo.de/)). Sign and submit  it with your supporting visa application documents. For more information, read  the user guide.
The application form has to be signed personally by the applicant (as  the applicant has signed their passport). For applicants under 18 years of age,  the application form must be signed by all parents or legal guardians.
Please make sure you have provided a valid e-mail address and phone  number in the application form. All further information regarding your visa  application (e.g., requests for additional documents/information) will be sent  to your e-mail address mentioned in the application form.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Attention: Booking appointments and handing in long-term/type D visa applications through VFS is currently only possible for applicants who have their habitual residence in the consular district of the Embassy in Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou and Chengdu (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/Jurisdiction).

Researchers are third-country nationals who
1. hold a doctoral degree or an appropriate higher education qualification giving access to doctoral programs, and
2. are selected by a research institution and admitted to Germany
3. in order to carry out a research activity for which such a degree is normally required.
This may also include doctoral students:
If the doctorate program is to be carried out within the framework of an employment contract or a research agreement with a German university or research institution (individual doctorate), a researcher visa must be applied for.
Note: Foreign doctoral candidates who have been admitted to a full-time doctoral program at a German university (i.e., structured doctorate, research is not the main focus of the stay) can apply for a visa to study for a doctorate in Germany. The structured doctorate includes a curriculum of courses, usually with an interdisciplinary focus. Further information can be obtained from the German university. The admitting university will decide whether a certificate from the Embassy's Academic Evaluation Office (Akademische Prüfstelle APS) is required for admission.
If your family members wish to accompany you, we recommend submitting joint applications. Every family member needs their own appointment. Please make sure to book appointments for the whole family on the same day if you wish to apply through VFS as VFS cannot accept individual applications of family members. If your family members want to apply for a reunification visa at a later point, they can book an appointment in the category "family reunion" at the visa section of the Embassy in Beijing, or the Consulate in Shanghai. 
Please refer to our information sheets on reunification visas for spouses and reunification visas of children (see “Documents Required”) (#long-researcher).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Skilled employees of an international company or enterprise who are traveling to Germany (a trainee or a trainer) do not require a work permit for a stay of a maximum of 90 days per year.
                                                                        Apply for your visa for internal training and provide the invitation letter from Germany according to § 17 BeschV, stating the training schedule in detail.
Foreign physicians who are temporarily staying in a German hospital within the scope of an advanced training programme/postgraduate medical education and are not practicing alternative
                                                                        medicine (“hands-off training”) do not require permission according to § 10 BÄO and are thus not exercising an occupation. Apply for your visa for a “hands-off training” and provide
                                                                        the invitation letter from the German clinic that invited you, specifying the training schedule in detail.
                                                                    
Internships might require a work permit issued by the “Bundesagentur für Arbeit”  German Federal Employment Agency (BA) (https://www.arbeitsagentur.de/). Before signing your intern contract, your German employer should
                                                                        contact the responsible local Bundesagentur für Arbeit for clarification.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Attention: Booking appointments and handing in long-term/type D visa applications for university studies through VFS is currently only possible for applicants who have their habitual residence in the consular district of the Embassy in Beijing and the Consulate General in Guangzhou (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/Jurisdiction).
When can I apply for a study visa?
Study visas can be applied for if you wish to
enrol at a university in Germany for a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program and you have already received an admission letter
take part in preparatory college (“Studienkolleg”) and you have already received an admission letter
attend a preparatory language course and you have either received a conditional admission letter or have verifiably been in contact with potential universities The language course must not be an online course. It must offer at least 18 hours of classes per week, last for at least three months and it must ensure that you will achieve the necessary language skills to enrol at university.
are applying to universities and need to take entrance exams

Who can hand in their application at VFS?
VFS only accepts study visa applications for the following applicants:
Beijing Jurisdiction:
students who do not require an APS certificate You can refer to the APS website or contact the APS directly to find out if you require an APS certificate: https://www.aps.org.cn/ (https://www.aps.org.cn/)
students who want to attend a university of music or arts and have received an APS Telefax or a K-procedure certificate (Bescheinigung im Künstlerverfahren)

All other applicants (i.e. those with an APS certificate) must register on the adequate waiting list through the online booking site of the Embassy (https://service2.diplo.de/rktermin/extern/choose_categoryList.do?locationCode=peki&realmId=12).
*Guangzhou Jurisdiction:
Accepts for both the following applicants*
applicants who do not require an APS certificate
applicants who have an APS certificate
How can I hand in my application through VFS?
Applications can be submitted online via the "Auslandsportal"/Consular Service Portal (https://digital.diplo.de/) . Required evidence or supporting documents can be uploaded as pdf files. Please kindly consult the provided guide for detailed instructions on the application process.
Attention: Art students must upload their APS Telefax/Künsterbescheinigung together with the university’s admission letter or invitation to an entrance exam.
After a preliminary check by VFS, you will receive an invitation to book an appointment at VFS. Please wait for the invitation to book an appointment. Do not book another type of appointment (e.g. for researchers). If the wrong type of appointment has been booked, the application might be refused.
The appointment at VFS is necessary for checking the original documents, submission of biometric data (fingerprints and photo) and, if necessary, payment of fees. The application will then be processed by the visa section of the Embassy.

are applying to universities and need to take entrance exams

Who can hand in their application at VFS?
VFS only accepts study visa applications for the following applicants:
Beijing Jurisdiction:
students who do not require an APS certificate You can refer to the APS website or contact the APS directly to find out if you require an APS certificate: https://www.aps.org.cn/ (https://www.aps.org.cn/)
students who want to attend a university of music or arts and have received an APS Telefax or a K-procedure certificate (Bescheinigung im Künstlerverfahren)

All other applicants (i.e. those with an APS certificate) must register on the adequate waiting list through the online booking site of the Embassy (https://service2.diplo.de/rktermin/extern/choose_categoryList.do?locationCode=peki&realmId=12).
*Guangzhou Jurisdiction:
Accepts for both the following applicants*
applicants who do not require an APS certificate
applicants who have an APS certificate
How can I hand in my application through VFS?
Applications can be submitted online via the "Auslandsportal"/Consular Service Portal (https://digital.diplo.de/) . Required evidence or supporting documents can be uploaded as pdf files. Please kindly consult the provided guide for detailed instructions on the application process.
Attention: Art students must upload their APS Telefax/Künsterbescheinigung together with the university’s admission letter or invitation to an entrance exam.
After a preliminary check by VFS, you will receive an invitation to book an appointment at VFS. Please wait for the invitation to book an appointment. Do not book another type of appointment (e.g. for researchers). If the wrong type of appointment has been booked, the application might be refused.
The appointment at VFS is necessary for checking the original documents, submission of biometric data (fingerprints and photo) and, if necessary, payment of fees. The application will then be processed by the visa section of the Embassy.

## Visa Fees
VFS Global is collecting three types of application fees:
- Visa fees, which are collected on behalf of the consular authority.
- Application Service fees, which relate to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.
- Added Value Service fees, which relate to
 
optional added value services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/premium-services)
 
you have used during the application process.
Please note: The payment of the above fees does not guarantee that a visa will be granted. The visa fee and the service fee are both non-refundable and non-transferable after the application is transferred to the consular authority, the visa application is denied by the administration or the applicant decides to terminate his or her application.
Visa fees

[ Table: Researcher (Beijing Jurisdiction only)]
The fee is fixed in Euro but payable in Renminbi only.
The exchange rate between EUR and CNY is decided by the Consular Authority and updated periodically, therefore the CNY amount you are requested to pay may differ from those quoted above.
Please note that the visa fee receipt is provided by the Consular Authority and the fee is indicated in EUR and CNY.
Application service fee
VFS GLOBAL Service fee mandatory for all visa applications is 34.13 EUR, 273 CNY per application inclusive of taxes. This fee corresponds to the standard application processing service provided by VFS (i.e. Information provision, appointment booking, visa application reception, data processing, etc.).

- 
## Visa fee exemptions
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Spouse, partner in civil union or underage child of German citizen
- Parents of underage German citizen
- Family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement
- Applicants who receive a publicly funded German scholarship for their stay in Germany
- 
## Mode of Payment
Mode of Payment:
- Counters accept payments by Cash/WeChat/POS/Alipay

## Documents Required
For details on required application documents, please consult the website of the German missions in China. Use the information provided for your specific visa type and prepare your application accordingly.
You can find information in German here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-de/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum/1209056). 
You can find information in Chinese here (https://china.diplo.de/cn-zh/service/visa-einreise/nationales-visum-1345434) .

## Photo Specifications
Photo
Please submit one passport photo which should fulfil the following criteria (conforming to ICAO standards).
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
                                                                    
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
- Photo should not be modified with Photoshop
The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfil the above criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area.
                                                                        For more details, please refer to the photo template. 
Photos that are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. To avoid
                                                                        this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
Processing time depends on the visa type, the involvement of local authorities in Germany, and the applicant’s individual circumstances. Some applications process within one to two weeks, while others may take several months.
Applicants should apply as early as possible, but no more than six months before their planned date of travel.
Applicants can rest assured that all applications are processed as quickly as possible. Should additional information or documents be required, applicants will be contacted directly. Applicants are therefore kindly asked to refrain from inquiring about their application status.

## Download Forms
National/long-term Visa Application Form
Please complete and print the following application form* (https://videx-national.diplo.de/ (https://videx-national.diplo.de/)). Sign and submit  it with your supporting visa application documents. For more information, read  the user guide.
The application form has to be signed personally by the applicant (as  the applicant has signed their passport). For applicants under 18 years of age,  the application form must be signed by all parents or legal guardians.
Please make sure you have provided a valid e-mail address and phone  number in the application form. All further information regarding your visa  application (e.g., requests for additional documents/information) will be sent  to your e-mail address mentioned in the application form.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS GLOBAL must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Your final destination is outside of the Schengen area and you need to travel via a German airport in order to reach your final destination? Then apply for a short-stay Schengen visa or an airport transit visa. 
                                                                        Please find the difference between a Schengen visa, a national visa and an airport transit visa in our FAQ list (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/faq).
An airport transit visa is required depending upon the country of your citizenship and whether you have residence permits or visas of certain countries.
AIRPORT TRANSIT - Your final travel destination is outside Germany /the Schengen area and your flight booking involves a transit time at a German airport below 24 hours to connect to your final destination.
Chinese citizens do not need an airport transit visa for a stay in the international transit area of a German airport. Only if you leave the international transit area (example: change of terminals or departure airport, or connecting flight destination within the Schengen area) Chinese citizens enter the Schengen area by leaving the transit zone and thus need a short stay Schengen visa.
Depending on your citizenship you might need to apply for an airport transit visa for a stay in the international transit area of the airport. 
For transit through a German airport, please check on the website of the European Commission (Visa policy - Migration and Home Affairs - European Commission (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/visa-policy_en)) if you need an airport transit visa. And check your travel itinerary and the circumstances of every airport with your travel agency or airline prior to your departure.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa category is applicable to all student groups, with accompanying teachers, who are invited by a German partner school for cultural exchange and joint class attendance. It is
                                                                        advised that all group members submit their applications together.
If you visit several schools in the context of mainly tourist group travel, please apply for a tourism visa (#tourism). If the journey is organized by a Chinese or German intermediary company, this
                                                                        information should be mentioned in your documents.
If underage children travel, special document (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/chn/en/deu/faq) proof is mandatory.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This category is applicable for business trips related to a trade fair in Germany.

## Trade fair visitor

Trade fair visitors do not need an original invitation letter from the German trade fair company or expo organizers. Please provide a detailed explanation letter issued by your employer. It should contain the correct name of the trade fair, location, travel period, reason for visiting the fair amongst the other relevant details. You might also provide additional documents such as entrance tickets or travel itinerary if you join an organized group travel. If you combine the visit of the trade fair with tourism / sightseeing, please provide itinerary and proof of accommodation as well. 

## Trade fair exhibitor
If your employer is exhibiting at a German trade fair, please provide the necessary documents as a trade fair exhibitor, even if you do not hold a personal exhibitor pass.

## Booth builders
Please provide proof about your employment, especially if hired by subcontractors, and for which Chinese exhibitor you will be doing booth building or dismantling work. False statements or documentation might lead to the refusal of your visa application.

##

For more information, please check the Premium services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/deu/premium-services) page.

## Visa fee exemptions
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Spouse, partner in civil union or underage child of German citizen
- Parents of underage German citizen
- Family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement
- School pupils, students, postgraduates students and accompanying teachers for study or educational training, if part of the local curriculum (proof has to be provided)
- Researchers traveling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Representatives of registered non-profit organizations aged max. 25 years for events organized by registered non-profit organizations

Overview
This category is applicable if you are invited by a German school/college/university to attend classes in Germany and your intended stay will be less than 90 days. Please always provide
                                                                        proof of the course programme and further details. The invitation letter should confirm whether course fees have already been paid.
An extension of this Schengen visa will not be possible. If you plan to enroll in a German university, you should apply for a long-term national visa for study purposes, not a Schengen
                                                                        visa.
                                                                    
Music and Art students scheduled for university entrance tests in Germany should also apply for a national visa for study purposes at the respective German mission (https://china.diplo.de/), instead of applying
                                                                        for a short study Schengen visa.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please note the following does not apply to family members of German citizens.
You can apply for this visa type if you are a spouse or partner in civil union, child, stepchild, grandchild under the age of 21 years of an EU/EEA citizen and, you are travelling together with an EU/EEA citizen.
If you intend to travel on your own at any time, you must apply for a regular visitor or tourist visa, and a regular visa fee will apply.
If you hold a Chinese passport and a passport of an EU/EEA country, you can directly enjoy the right of free movement and can enter Germany with your EU/EEA passport
Please note that a Schengen visa only allows you to stay in Germany for a maximum of 90 days within a period of 180 days. It is not possible to change a Schengen visa into a permanent
                                                                        residence permit upon arrival. If you are intending a permanent stay in Germany, please apply for a national visa (https://china.diplo.de/) at the respective German mission.

Please complete and print the following application form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/desktop/index.html). Sign and submit it with your supporting visa application documents. For more information, read the user guide or click here (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/desktop/index.html) to
                                                                        access the launch assistant directly.
The application form has to be signed personally by the applicant. For applicants under 18 years of age, the application form must be signed at least by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
                                                                    
Please make sure you have provided a valid e-mail address in the application form. All further information regarding your visa application (e.g., requests for additional documents/information)
                                                                        will be sent to your e-mail address mentioned in the application form. 
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here
